name: Pad Thai (Sauteed Rice Noodles)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, soak the rice noodles in 10 to 12 cups of cold water for 2 hours. Drain and cover with a damp towel to retain moisture.
2. In a wok or large frying pan, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Stir-fry the garlic until it is light brown. Add the shrimp, bean curd, and pickled turnips; stir in the vinegar, fish sauce, paprika, and sugar. When thoroughly mixed, fold in the noodles.
3. Spread the noodles out to the sides of the wok or frying pan, leaving a space in the middle. Add the beaten eggs. As the eggs cook, fold them over the noodles and stir to combine all ingredients evenly.
4. Stir in half of the mung bean sprouts, then add the scallions, ground peanuts, and chopped chili pepper. Toss several times to mix well.
5. Serve on a large platter with lemon wedges. Top with the remaining bean sprouts and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve crushed red pepper on the side for those who like it extra-spicy.
